div.p: before: { label: "Submission Phase: Asynchronous Batching" style.stroke-width: 2 style.fill: "#F8F9FA"
sq_ring: { label: "Submission Queue (SQ) - 64B SQEs (Cache Line Aligned)" style.fill: "#E8F4FD" style.stroke: "#2E86AB"
div.h1: Fixed the near key by attaching it to the root shape label
div.p: barrier: "IO_DRAIN\nBARRIER" { shape: rectangle width: 80 height: 450 style.fill: "#FF6B35" style.stroke: "#CC5500" style.stroke-width: 4 style.font-color: white style.bold: true style.font-size: 18 label.near: center }
after: { label: "Completion Phase: Ordered Retirement" style.stroke-width: 2 style.fill: "#F0F4F0"
cq_ring: { label: "Completion Queue (CQ) - 16B CQEs" style.fill: "#F0FFF0" style.stroke: "#228B22"
t1 -> t2 -> t3 -> t4: {style.stroke: "#666"; style.stroke-width: 2} }
insight: ||md

ul.li: Ordering: All SQEs submitted prior to the DRAIN SQE must complete before the DRAIN SQE starts.
Memory Ordering: Provides a full memory barrier for prior completions.
Use Case: Ensuring file writes are metadata-consistent before a final NOP or sync operation.
Byte Offset: SQEs are strictly 64-bytes, CQEs are 16-bytes.

div.p: | {near: bottom-center}
perf_box: { label: "Throughput/Latency Metrics" style.fill: "#FFF0F0" near: bottom-right
grid-columns: 1
